steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage explains that longitudinal studies like NILS are expensive because they span many years. Public opinion studies don't need that multi-year approach — so figure out what follows for their cost.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘the public opinion study could likely be completed at a considerably lower cost than NILS.’: If public opinion studies don't require the time-intensive longitudinal methods that drive NILS's cost, they should cost much less. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘longitudinal methods would likely be less suitable for studies in public opinion than for studies in public health.’: This reverses the cost direction — without the multi-year burden, cost goes down, not up. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘longitudinal methods would likely be less expensive for studies in public opinion than for studies in public health.’: The passage draws a direct contrast; skipping the multi-year method removes the cost driver. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘the results from NILS would likely be more reliable than those from the public opinion study.’: The passage never compares reliability between the two study types. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
